data.title: Stablecoins Face Growth Cap Under GENIUS Act, Economist Issues Warning
data.description:
Key Insights: Berenberg economist Atakan Bakiskan argued that stablecoins face structural limitations that prevent significant growth, despite new federal regulations designed to legitimize the sector. Bakiskan stated that stablecoins were unlikely to absorb substantial US government debt or compete effectively with bank deposits.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/11/20/stablecoins-face-growth-cap-under-genius-act-economist-issues-warning/

data.title: Market News Today: Fed December Rate Cut Odds Collapse to 30% as Data Blackout and Market Fear Intensify
data.description: Odds of a Federal Reserve rate cut at the December FOMC meeting have plunged to 30%, sharply lower than the 50% probability seen just a day ago and far below the 98% conviction from one month earlier, according to CME’s FedWatch tool. The collapse comes amid a growing economic data blackout driven by the recent government shutdown and deep divisions within the central bank.What to KnowThe probability of a December Fed rate cut is now 30%, down from 50% last week and 98% in October.The Labor Department will not release October jobs data due to the government shutdown.Fed minutes show sharp disagreements among policymakers on whether to pause or reduce rates.Bitcoin briefly traded around $91,700, up 0.8% despite broader market fear.Prediction markets show rate-cut odds between 33% and 70%, depending on the platform.Fed December Rate Cut Odds Fall as Data Blackout DeepensThe dramatic drop in Fed December rate cut odds highlights the growing difficulty for policymakers as they prepare for the final FOMC meeting of the year. The Labor Department confirmed it will not release October nonfarm payrolls, marking the longest government data blackout in modern U.S. history.Without fresh employment data, inflation readings, or key economic indicators, analysts warn the Federal Reserve may be forced to rely on incomplete information when deciding whether to cut rates for the first time in the current cycle.Minutes from the October meeting released Wednesday show a divided Fed, with several officials arguing for a pause while others favored a 25 bps cut.Prediction Markets Show Growing UncertaintyCME FedWatch now places rate-cut odds at 30%, while prediction platforms show slightly more optimistic—but still shaky—probabilities:Kalshi: 70%Polymarket: 67%CME: 30%The divergence highlights the market’s struggle to price a December move without reliable macroeconomic data.According to analyst commentary from The Kobeissi Letter, traders remain hesitant due to lingering inflation risk and uncertainty about the Fed’s tolerance for easing prematurely.Crypto Market Hit by Fear as Bitcoin Slips Below $89,000The collapse in rate-cut expectations has intensified volatility across crypto markets. Bitcoin fell below $89,000 on Wednesday, failing to defend a major support region and extending a multi-day slide below its 365-day moving average.Adding to bearish sentiment:BTC’s 50-day EMA crossed below the 200-day EMA, forming a classic “death cross.”Crypto Fear & Greed Index fell to 16, signaling “extreme fear”—just one point above its yearly low.Some analysts now warn this could mark the start of a deeper crypto downturn if macro and liquidity conditions continue to tighten.Analysts Warn of Potential Drop to $75,000 Before RecoveryMarket strategist Benjamin Cowen said Bitcoin must rebound within the next week to avoid a steeper decline:“The time for Bitcoin to bounce, if the cycle is not over, would start within the next week,” Cowen said Sunday.“If no bounce occurs, we likely see another dump before a larger rally back to the 200-day simple moving average, which would then mark a macro lower high.”Several analysts point to a possible retest of $75,000 as Bitcoin searches for a long-term support floor.Why the Fed’s December Decision Matters for Crypto and Risk AssetsA Fed rate cut would:Lower borrowing costsBoost liquiditySupport risk-on assets like cryptoBut with data uncertainty, policy disagreements, and inflation concerns, the odds now favor a longer wait before the Fed begins easing.Macro analysts say markets may remain highly volatile through year-end, especially without upcoming economic releases to guide expectations.

data.title: Crypto News Today: Nvidia Earnings Beat and Strong Outlook Calm Jittery Markets; Bitcoin Reclaims $90K
data.description: Nvidia delivered a blockbuster third-quarter earnings report and stronger-than-expected guidance on Wednesday, easing fears of an AI bubble and stabilizing tech and crypto markets after a volatile week. Bitcoin, which dropped below $88,000 earlier in the day, surged back above $90,000 as risk appetite improved.What to KnowNvidia beat Q3 earnings expectations and issued a stronger-than-expected Q4 revenue outlook.CEO Jensen Huang said “Blackwell sales are off the charts, and cloud GPUs are sold out.”The results briefly calmed jittery markets, with Bitcoin rebounding from intraday lows.AI-linked crypto tokens rallied 4–5% in after-hours trading.Nvidia shares rose 4% after hours, while AI-pivoting mining stocks also jumped.Nvidia Posts Record Q3 Revenue as AI Demand SurgesNvidia reported record third-quarter revenue of $57.01 billion, a 62% year-over-year increase, beating Wall Street’s $54.7 billion expectation. Profit rose 65% to $31.9 billion, also ahead of projections, reinforcing Nvidia’s position as the central hardware supplier for the accelerating AI boom.“Blackwell sales are off the charts, and cloud GPUs are sold out,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said following the earnings release. “Compute demand keeps accelerating and compounding across training and inference — each growing exponentially.”Nvidia shares rose 4% in after-hours trading.Data Center Revenue Outperforms ExpectationsThe company’s data center division, the backbone of its AI business, generated $51.2 billion in Q3 revenue, topping consensus forecasts of $49.34 billion. Demand for advanced GPUs across cloud infrastructure, hyperscalers, research labs, and large language model developers continues to exceed supply.Nvidia’s Q4 Outlook: Even StrongerNvidia expects fourth-quarter revenue of $63.7–$66.3 billion, well above analysts’ expectations of $62 billion. The guidance suggests the AI infrastructure spending wave remains intact despite broader market fears.The chipmaker will hold a conference call at 5 p.m. ET, where investors expect commentary on next-generation GPU supply, enterprise demand, and future AI-compute roadmaps.AI-Linked Crypto Tokens Surge After Nvidia’s BeatRisk appetite flowed quickly into digital assets tied to AI infrastructure and machine-learning ecosystems.Following the earnings release:TAO rose 4%Near Protocol (NEAR) climbed 4%Internet Computer (ICP) rose 5%Render (RNDR) gained 5%These tokens had been under pressure amid growing speculation of an AI bubble, but Nvidia’s results helped reverse sentiment.Bitcoin Rebounds Above $90K After Losing Key SupportBitcoin (BTC) fell to $88,540 earlier Wednesday — its first dip below $89,000 since April — before rebounding above $90,000 following Nvidia’s report. BTC last traded around $91,904, according to market data.The recovery comes despite:A 10% decline in Bitcoin over the past weekBTC trading below its 365-day moving averageA bearish death cross, with the 50-day EMA falling below the 200-day EMACrypto markets had been rattled by risk-off sentiment, macro uncertainty, and declining tech stocks. Nvidia’s strong earnings temporarily eased those pressures.Ether (ETH) also recovered above $3,000 after dropping to its lowest level since July.AI-Focused Mining Stocks Also JumpBitcoin mining companies with AI infrastructure offerings rallied sharply in after-hours trading:IREN: +8%Cipher Mining (CIFR): +11%Hut 8 Mining (HUT): +6%These miners have positioned themselves as AI data-center providers, a strategy that helped them surge earlier this year before the recent sector selloff.Big Tech Gains in After-Hours TradingNvidia’s results lifted the broader tech sector:Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN) and Meta (META) all traded higher after the bell.Crypto-adjacent equities like Coinbase (COIN), MicroStrategy (MSTR) and Circle Internet Group (CRCL) saw modest gains after closing the day lower.Crypto exchange Bullish (BLSH) rose 1% after hours following its strongest earnings quarter since going public.Why Nvidia’s Earnings Matter for Crypto and MarketsMarkets viewed Nvidia’s results as a critical gauge of whether the AI boom is entering bubble territory or still expanding. For now, the report suggests:AI spending remains robustGPU demand continues to exceed supplyEnterprise investment is acceleratingThat narrative reassured investors, lifting both tech and crypto assets that had been sliding over the past several weeks.

data.title: XRP News: First U.S. Spot XRP ETF Could Go Live on Thursday as Canary Funds Nears Final Approval
data.description: The First U.S. Spot XRP ETF Could Go Live on Thursday, marking a major development for the digital asset industry as Canary Funds moves closer to launching the nation’s first pure spot XRP exchange-traded fund. The move could significantly reshape institutional access to XRP and broaden the crypto ETF landscape beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum.Canary Funds filed Form 8-A with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Tuesday, signaling that the product has completed its registration requirements and is ready for listing. According to Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas, the filing represents the final regulatory step before trading and typically precedes activation within 24 hours.The ETF will become effective once Nasdaq certifies the listing, a decision expected by 5:30 p.m. ET on Wednesday, putting the fund on track for a potential Thursday market debut.A New Milestone for XRP and U.S. Crypto ETFsIf approved, the launch would be the first pure spot XRP ETF available to U.S. investors, offering direct exposure to XRP under the Securities Act of 1933. This structure allows investors to hold an asset-backed product that tracks the market price of XRP without relying on futures or blended-asset strategies.The ETF’s debut comes nearly two years after the highly anticipated arrival of spot Bitcoin ETFs in January 2024 and follows the approval of spot Ether ETFs earlier this year. Crypto analysts believe this marks a new phase in the U.S. ETF market—one where alternative networks beyond the top two assets gain regulatory traction.Institutional Demand Could Drive Strong InflowsA successful launch could draw fresh inflows from registered investment advisers (RIAs) who have avoided direct crypto exposure due to custody, compliance, or risk constraints. A spot ETF structure gives advisers a regulated investment wrapper while avoiding the operational complexities of holding XRP on-chain.Market observers also expect the product to improve liquidity and price discovery, since ETF shares must be backed by actual spot XRP held in custody by a regulated trust.A Cleaner, Fully Backed Alternative to XRPR ETFCanary’s offering contrasts sharply with the recently launched REX-Osprey XRPR ETF, which provides only partial XRP exposure under the Investment Company Act of 1940. XRPR holds a mixed basket of assets rather than maintaining full one-to-one XRP backing.Because of this structure, XRPR faces:Higher tracking varianceLess efficient tax treatmentLimited appeal among institutional allocatorsCanary’s ETF, by comparison, will be 100% XRP-backed, offering a cleaner and more transparent structure for investors seeking pure exposure.Market Reaction and Broader Crypto ImplicationsXRP traded near $2.48 in Asian markets early Wednesday, down roughly 5% amid a broader crypto pullback. Analysts expect that confirmation of Thursday’s launch could catalyze renewed trading activity.Beyond immediate price movements, the ETF’s approval could signal rising regulatory openness toward alternative crypto assets, with Solana ETF applications already under review. If successful, XRP’s debut could pave the way for more diversified blockchain-based investment products.Analysts Watching for ConfirmationBalchunas noted that previous filings—such as Canary’s recent HBAR ETF—went live the day after Form 8-A submission. While not guaranteed, the pattern strongly suggests that the First U.S. Spot XRP ETF Could Go Live on Thursday.Market participants are now watching for Nasdaq’s final certification, the last administrative hurdle before trading begins.

data.title: Ethereum News: BlackRock Files New iShares Staked Ethereum Trust as Race for Staked ETH ETFs Heats Up
data.description: BlackRock has officially taken its first step toward launching a Staked Ether ETF, registering the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust in Delaware in a move that signals growing competition among issuers seeking approval for yield-bearing Ethereum products.The filing, first highlighted by Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas, indicates BlackRock’s intent to enter the staked ETH market at a time when issuers await formal guidance from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on whether staking can be included in U.S.-listed ETFs.What to knowBlackRock has registered the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust in Delaware.The filing is not yet a formal ETF application under the Securities Act of 1933.BlackRock’s move follows VanEck’s similar registration tied to Lido’s staked ETH.Issuers continue to wait for SEC clarity on including staking in spot ETH ETFs.Staked ETH ETFs could offer yield-bearing returns, with staking currently averaging 3.95% annually.BlackRock Registers iShares Staked Ethereum Trust in DelawareThe registration, filed on Nov. 19, does not constitute an official ETF proposal but is widely viewed as the first administrative step toward seeking SEC approval. The trust would enable BlackRock to launch a spot-based, yield-generating Staked Ether ETF, pending regulatory clarity.Balchunas noted that a filing under the Securities Act of 1933 positions the product for full asset backing, transparency requirements, and traditional ETF disclosure standards.“BlackRock is planning to file for a Staked Ethereum ETF, as per the Delaware name registration. ’33 Act. Filing coming soon,”— Eric Balchunas, BloombergWhy the Filing MattersStaking remains one of the largest unresolved regulatory questions in the U.S. crypto ETF market. While spot ETH ETFs launched in 2024, the SEC required issuers to remove staking features, arguing they could constitute unregistered securities offerings.With a more crypto-friendly SEC under the current administration and new generic ETF listing standards that accelerate approvals, issuers believe the window for staking-enabled products may finally be opening.VanEck, REX-Osprey, and Grayscale Already in the RaceBlackRock’s move follows VanEck’s registration of a staked ETH trust tied to Lido-staked ETH (stETH), as well as staked ETH ETF launches by REX-Osprey and Grayscale in recent months.Roughly 70 crypto-linked products are now awaiting SEC action after delays caused by the U.S. government shutdown earlier this fall.The competitive landscape suggests issuers expect strong demand for yield-enhanced Ethereum products.How a Staked Ether ETF Would WorkA Staked Ether ETF would deliver two return streams:ETH price appreciationStaking yield, currently averaging about 3.95% per year (Blocknative)This converts the product into a total-return ETF, broadening its appeal for:Yield-focused investorsIncome-driven fundsRegistered investment advisers (RIAs) seeking predictable returnsBy offering a built-in yield, a Staked ETH ETF could attract investors who avoided first-wave ETH ETFs due to their lack of income generation.BlackRock’s ETHA Strategy and Next StepsBlackRock already manages the iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA), which launched in July 2024 and has attracted $13.1 billion in inflows. ETHA currently does not support staking.On its website, BlackRock previously stated:“Staking involves operational complexities and regulatory issues that currently make it unfeasible.”However, in July the firm joined other issuers in submitting a proposed SEC rule change that would allow ETH staking in existing spot ETF products.The new staked ETH trust would effectively serve as a dedicated vehicle for a yield-bearing Ethereum ETF once regulators provide clarity.Market Outlook: A New Phase for Crypto ETFsBlackRock’s move suggests a growing consensus that staking will eventually be permitted in U.S.-listed ETFs. Analysts say a Staked Ether ETF could:Increase total returnsImprove demand for Ethereum productsBroaden ETF adoption among income-focused investorsMark the next evolution of digital-asset ETFs after Bitcoin and ETH spot approvalsWith competition rising and issuers positioning early, the Staked ETH category may become one of the most significant battlegrounds in the 2025 crypto ETF market.

data.title: Metaplanet issues $150 million in Class B perpetual preferred stock to expand its Bitcoin reserve strategy.
data.description: Metaplanet CEO Simon Gerovich posted on the X platform that Metaplanet announced the launch of $150 million in Class B perpetual preferred stock MERCURY, with a fixed dividend of 4.9% and a conversion price of 1,000 yen. This move is aimed at expanding its Bitcoin reserve strategy.

data.title: Binance: The Binance Alpha airdrop will launch today at 20:00 (UTC+8), with a minimum requirement of 240 Alpha Credits.
data.description: Binance Wallet announced that the Binance Alpha airdrop will launch today at 20:00 (UTC+8). Users with at least 240 Binance Alpha Credits can claim tokens on a first-come, first-served basis until the airdrop pool is exhausted or the airdrop event ends. More details will be announced soon. Furthermore, to ensure the best experience during the Alpha event, please update your Binance App to the latest version (3.4.0 or above) before 00:00 (UTC) on November 21, 2025. Older versions will not be eligible for subsequent Alpha events.

data.title: Samourai Wallet CTO Gets 4 Years in Prison For $2B Bitcoin Laundering Scheme
data.description:
William “Bill” Hill, CTO and co-founder of Samourai Wallet, has been sentenced to four years in prison for running an unlicensed crypto mixing service that facilitated over $237 million in criminal proceeds
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/samourai-wallet-cto-prison-bitcoin-laundering/
